comments.post_body:
Xfinity (aka Comcast) has been causing so much trouble with my Internet connection. The price constantly goes up, and the quality regularly goes down. Today it was particularly terrible; I've been losing connection every 5 mins in the evening and every ~20 earlier during the day.

I know if I complain, they will use any potential reason to blame me for the issues (as it happened many times already): not their modem, not their router, connection to device not wired, etc. The only thing I didn't verify yet, is whether the connection was bad at the input to my Wi-Fi router, or my router is the problem (very unlikely, since it had worked fine for years). Hence, I decided to do that verification, too, before calling Xfinity.

I wanted a script to check the connection and log any interruptions. I will connect a machine with an ethernet cable directly to my modem and run that script for a day or two, and then check the logs for issues.

Long story short, I just want to show how useful the AI assistants (ChatGpt, Copilot) can be. Here's what I got in 2 mins; will start my testing tomorrow:
comments.context_summary: The commenter is experiencing frequent internet connection losses with Xfinity and is preparing to troubleshoot the issue by running a script to log interruptions, before potentially contacting the provider. They are highlighting the usefulness of AI assistants in quickly generating the necessary script to aid in their troubleshooting efforts.

comments.comment_body: My recommendation is read fiction. All these motivational/inspirational books are just telling you to do this and that. You can get a good 2-page summary for each from an LLM. But reading a good fiction is like listening the author tell it to you; no LLM can tell it like the author, and removing a single sentence might change the impression. Finally, fiction is the only category where the truth is told without the need to be politically correct and buzzword-laden.
comments.post_body:
After a couple of years of barely reading, I finally got back into the habit last year. I read around 15 books in total, and I want share my top 8 - in the order I read them. I’ve also included a few quotes I liked to give you a quick glimpse into each book.

1️⃣ The Magic of Thinking Big — David J. Schwartz 9.5/10
A book recommended by a friend, Davit Abgaryan, PhD. 
A book to recalibrate your internal standards.
Quote: “Your most important product is yourself. Never sell yourself short.”

2️⃣ The ONE Thing — Gary W. Keller & Jay Papasan 10/10
Possibly the most actionable book I read. The question that stayed with me all year:
Quote: “What’s the ONE thing I can do such that by doing it, everything else will be easier or unnecessary?”

3️⃣ The 12 Week Year — Brian Moran & Michael Lennington 10/10
Very inspiring, packed with practical takeaways. 
Quote: "Many people spend a significant amount of time and energy attempting to eliminate their weaknesses, while it is the focusing on your strengths that produces your greatest achievements." 

4️⃣ Extreme Ownership & Discipline Equals Freedom — Jocko Willink & Leif Babin  9/10 
Read them in that order to increase the effect! 
Quote: "There is no excuse for not getting out of bed, especially when all rests on that decisive moment."

5️⃣ The Power of Positive Thinking — Norman Vincent Peale 10/10
The first half of the book was a bit challenging to get through, but after that, it became absolutely AMAZING. It even inspired me to start reading the Old and New Testaments.
Quote: "One of the greatest tragedies of the average person is the tendency to spend our whole lives, perfecting our faults." 

6️⃣ Outlive — Peter Attia. 8.5 /10
A wake-up call for anyone who’s crossed the 30-year mark. Start prioritizing your health today — your future self will thank you!

7️⃣ Areté — Brian Johnson.  9/10
Plenty of valuable insights from numerous brilliant thinkers and references, if you want to explore further.
Quote: "To thine own self be true. Trust yourself. Every heart vibrates to that iron string."

8️⃣ The Miracle Morning — Hal Elrod   8.5/10
Structure your morning. Shape your life.
Quote: "Life stays the same because we stay the same."

Share your top 9/10 or 10/10 books from recent years — I’d love to see your recommendations
comments.context_summary: The commenter is sharing their top 8 book recommendations from the past year, including self-help and personal development titles, and is highlighting key quotes and takeaways from each book. The commenter is responding to their own post, where they listed and reviewed the books they read.

comments.comment_body:
Arsén Y. M. I am actually missing the fiction part. Need to start reading fiction too!
I will argue that the way even non fiction books present their material is also very important. A two page summary will be very bleak and the effect most probably will be short lived. To understand it properly, one must first understand the details well enough to be able to simplify them into two pages.

comments.post_body:
🚀 Almost 7 years ago, I joined Krisp as a Software Engineer and helped launch our very first desktop app for Windows with a single feature (noise cancellation). Today, Krisp runs on 5 platforms with more than 5 huge and impactful features — and I’m stepping into the role of Director of Engineering - Desktop Applications.

Every step of this journey has been thrilling: learning how to build, how to lead, and how to believe in the impact we can create together.

I’m truly inspired every day by the people at Krisp and by our mission. We’re not just building technology — we’re changing the way the world communicates. And being part of that change is the most rewarding part of my career.

Here’s to the next chapter of building, learning, and making a difference with Krisp.
comments.context_summary: The commenter is congratulating the author on their new role as Director of Engineering - Desktop Applications at Krisp, a company that has grown significantly since they joined as a Software Engineer 7 years ago. The author is reflecting on their journey and expressing their excitement for the company's mission to change the way the world communicates.

comments.post_body:
✨ 𝑆𝑜𝑚𝑒𝑡𝑖𝑚𝑒𝑠 𝑦𝑜𝑢 𝑑𝑜𝑛’𝑡 𝑐ℎ𝑜𝑜𝑠𝑒 𝐥𝐞𝐚𝐝𝐞𝐫𝐬𝐡𝐢𝐩… 𝐥𝐞𝐚𝐝𝐞𝐫𝐬𝐡𝐢𝐩 𝑐ℎ𝑜𝑜𝑠𝑒𝑠 𝑦𝑜𝑢.

𝐘𝐞𝐬𝐭𝐞𝐫𝐝𝐚𝐲, 𝐈 𝐡𝐚𝐝 𝐚 “𝐥𝐢𝐟𝐞-𝐜𝐡𝐚𝐧𝐠𝐢𝐧𝐠” 𝐬𝐲𝐧𝐞𝐫𝐠𝐲 𝐬𝐞𝐬𝐬𝐢𝐨𝐧 (𝑎𝑘𝑎 𝑐𝑜𝑓𝑓𝑒𝑒) 𝑤𝑖𝑡ℎ 𝑚𝑦𝑠𝑒𝑙𝑓 𝑎𝑛𝑑 𝑟𝑒𝑎𝑙𝑖𝑧𝑒𝑑 𝑡ℎ𝑎𝑡 𝑠𝑢𝑐𝑐𝑒𝑠𝑠 𝑖𝑠 𝑛𝑜𝑡 𝑎𝑏𝑜𝑢𝑡 𝑤𝑜𝑟𝑘𝑖𝑛𝑔 ℎ𝑎𝑟𝑑 — 𝑖𝑡’𝑠 𝑎𝑏𝑜𝑢𝑡 𝐰𝐨𝐫𝐤𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐬𝐦𝐚𝐫𝐭… 𝑏𝑢𝑡 𝑎𝑙𝑠𝑜 𝐰𝐨𝐫𝐤𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐡𝐚𝐫𝐝… 𝑏𝑢𝑡 𝑠𝑚𝑎𝑟𝑡𝑒𝑟. 𝑌𝑜𝑢 𝑘𝑛𝑜𝑤 𝑤ℎ𝑎𝑡 𝐼 𝑚𝑒𝑎𝑛.

𝐈𝐧 𝐣𝐮𝐬𝐭 𝐭𝐡𝐫𝐞𝐞 𝐝𝐚𝐲𝐬, 𝐈 𝐦𝐚𝐧𝐚𝐠𝐞𝐝 𝐭𝐨 𝐞𝐦𝐩𝐨𝐰𝐞𝐫 𝐜𝐫𝐨𝐬𝐬-𝐟𝐮𝐧𝐜𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧𝐚𝐥 𝐢𝐧𝐧𝐨𝐯𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐛𝐲 𝐬𝐞𝐧𝐝𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐚𝐧 𝐞𝐦𝐚𝐢𝐥 𝐰𝐢𝐭𝐡 𝐭𝐰𝐨 𝐛𝐮𝐥𝐥𝐞𝐭 𝐩𝐨𝐢𝐧𝐭𝐬 — 𝐚𝐧𝐝 ℎ𝑜𝑛𝑒𝑠𝑡𝑙𝑦, 𝑡ℎ𝑒 𝑖𝑚𝑝𝑎𝑐𝑡 ℎ𝑎𝑠 𝑏𝑒𝑒𝑛 𝑛𝑜𝑡ℎ𝑖𝑛𝑔 𝑠ℎ𝑜𝑟𝑡 𝑜𝑓 𝐭𝐫𝐚𝐧𝐬𝐟𝐨𝐫𝐦𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧𝐚𝐥.

𝐏𝐞𝐨𝐩𝐥𝐞 𝐤𝐞𝐞𝐩 𝐚𝐬𝐤𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐦𝐞: “𝐇𝐨𝐰 𝐝𝐨 𝐲𝐨𝐮 𝐬𝐭𝐚𝐲 𝐬𝐨 𝐝𝐫𝐢𝐯𝐞𝐧?”
𝑻ℎ𝑒 𝑠𝑒𝑐𝑟𝑒𝑡 𝑖𝑠 𝑠𝑖𝑚𝑝𝑙𝑒: 𝐟𝐚𝐢𝐥 𝐟𝐚𝐬𝐭, 𝐢𝐭𝐞𝐫𝐚𝐭𝐞 𝐟𝐚𝐬𝐭𝐞𝐫, 𝑎𝑛𝑑 𝑜𝑐𝑐𝑎𝑠𝑖𝑜𝑛𝑎𝑙𝑙𝑦 𝐧𝐚𝐩 𝐬𝐭𝐫𝐚𝐭𝐞𝐠𝐢𝐜𝐚𝐥𝐥𝐲.

𝐋𝐞𝐭’𝐬 𝐫𝐞𝐦𝐞𝐦𝐛𝐞𝐫: 𝑖𝑡’𝑠 𝑛𝑜𝑡 𝑎𝑏𝑜𝑢𝑡 𝑡ℎ𝑒 𝐝𝐞𝐬𝐭𝐢𝐧𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝑂𝑅 𝑡ℎ𝑒 𝐣𝐨𝐮𝐫𝐧𝐞𝐲.
 𝐼𝑡’𝑠 𝑎𝑏𝑜𝑢𝑡 𝑡ℎ𝑒 𝐧𝐚𝐫𝐫𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐯𝐞 𝑦𝑜𝑢 𝑐𝑟𝑎𝑓𝑡 𝑜𝑛 𝐿𝑖𝑛𝑘𝑒𝑑𝐼𝑛 𝑎𝑓𝑡𝑒𝑟𝑤𝑎𝑟𝑑. 🚀

(read the first comment to learn more)

#Leadership #Innovation #Disruptor #GrowthMindset #Synergy

They emphasize that success is not about the destination or journey, but about the narrative you craft on LinkedIn afterwards.

comments.post_body:
Today marks 5 years at AWS OpenSearch! I’m grateful for the opportunities, challenges, and amazing people I've met along the way.

Starting as an intern, I’ve evolved through hands-on work with distributed systems and developing impactful solutions that empower businesses to leverage search and analytics. Each day has brought valuable learning experiences. Being involved in an open-source community has been both rewarding and energizing.

Thank you to my colleagues, mentors, and the OpenSearch community for their support and collaboration. Here's to more growth, innovation, and making a difference together.

Looking forward to more years of teamwork and progress!
#AWS #OpenSearch #Anniversary #Gratitude #OpenSource
comments.context_summary: The commenter is celebrating their 5-year anniversary at AWS OpenSearch, expressing gratitude for the opportunities and people they've met, and looking forward to continued growth and innovation. They're responding to their own post, marking the milestone and thanking their colleagues and the OpenSearch community for their support.

comments.post_body:
After almost 10 years at Microsoft, its time to take a pause and do something different. 

Rather than a short LinkedIn post, sharing the news and the learnings at this incredible company as a LinkedIn article.

comments.comment_body: Your comment brings me to another idea: we need some product for bringing the packages from outside to inside, something like a belt conveyor or a vacuum tube lift :))))
comments.post_body:
Random idea for reducing waste:

Imagine going on Amazon retail website (or any other eCommerce giant's), ordering something, and in shipping details picking an option "Ship in original box" or "Do not package". This could be eligible for majority of items that are already packaged in their factory boxes. So then Amazon would just print the shipping label on the original box and send it out. Maybe I am missing something, but I think as long as the label is on a flat surface and legible, the color of the box shouldn't matter. Imagine how many millions of cardboard boxes this will save.

I am sure today millions of people just open the Amazon box (the brown cardboard one), take out the item in its original box, and then get rid of the shipping box (hopefully everyone recycles, but who knows). So not sure what extra role that extra box plays other than taking more space, wasting more effort, and taking a lot of trees down. Yeah, I know, there is privacy, extra protection, etc. but those are minor, plus that's why I say the website should allow both choices with and w/o extra packaging.

Now finally, some items come in non-cardboard/plastic packaging, which is recyclable only in special facilities, and most of these end up in landfills. Even better reason for providing this option.
comments.context_summary: The commenter is responding to a post about reducing waste by introducing an option for customers to choose 'ship in original box' or 'do not package' when ordering from eCommerce websites like Amazon, potentially saving millions of cardboard boxes. This idea aims to minimize packaging waste and promote sustainability.

comments.post_body:
Dear network, I am writing you with a request to share with me the Companies, which can offer an internship in the following spheres: Web, Java, iOS development.
My friend is an Artsakh war veteran and ended military service earlier because of the wounds got during the war. He is a beginner in this area, has some basic knowledge but is enough motivated and eager to learn more.
Any help will be appreciated. 
Thanks in advance.

The commenter is responding to this request for assistance in finding internship opportunities.

comments.post_body:
Հայերենից ո՞նց եք։

Չէ, դասավանդման մակարդակի չէ։ Ուղղակի, եթե ՀԱՅԵՐԵՆ ինչ-որ բառ կամ բառակապակցություն ասեն, կհասկանա՞ք։

Եթե՝ ԱՅՈ, ուրեմն հիմա կարդացեք հետևյալ բառացանկը ու թարգմանեք ինձ համար, խնդրում եմ․․․․

1. մատչում - 
2. նշիչ - 
3. տեղեկադարան - 
4. հեռապատճեն - 
5. տոմսածրար - 
6. գործունացում - 
7. հարմարիչ -
8. միջերեսակ - 
9. ընդմիջարկել - 
10. դիտանցել - 
11. ձևակազմ - 
12. թափարգել - 
13. լարք - 
14. գործուն խտարար - 


Ազնվությամբ՝ ես էլ չգիտեմ ճիշտ թարգմանությունները։
Եկեք տեսնենք, թե քանիսն ենք կարողանում հասկանալ։
Ով ավելի շատ ))))))))))

comments.comment_body: Լավ, տեսա արդեն պատասխանը
comments.post_body:
🚀Today's the big day! We've built the ultimate mobile noise-canceling app for business calls - Krisp for iOS. 

It's a revolutionary meeting and mobile dialer equipped with our AI-powered noise cancellation. This app will enhance your phone with superpowers  and you will be able to do all these cool things:

✅ Enjoy noise-free business calls and conference meetings
✅ Connect to your Webex, Zoom or Hangouts meetings with one click
✅ Auto-sync with your calendar
✅ Make calls to any phone numbers
✅ Use the app from anywhere

Now, why is it revolutionary?
Built-in noise cancellation in phones is poor, and it even stops working when you use a headset or AirPods.

With Krisp, you can hold your phone however you want and use a headset as much as you want, the noise cancellation will still work like a charm!

P.S. Come give us a hug on Product Hunt! 
#growth #producthunt
comments.context_summary: The commenter is responding to the announcement of Krisp, a mobile noise-canceling app for business calls on iOS, which features AI-powered noise cancellation and seamless integration with meeting platforms. The app aims to revolutionize mobile business calls by providing effective noise cancellation, even with headsets or AirPods.

comments.comment_body: Reminds me of the secret red folder from the Dilbert comics: https://dilbert.com/strip/2017-01-20

If you know the answer but don't know how to get to that answer, it will be obvious. If you know how to get to the answer, then you can just do that. So in any case, do yourself a favor and never rely on a cheat sheet.
comments.post_body: Sorting algorithms time complexity for reference.

comments.parent_comment_body: ARman Atoyan որ կարողանաք Երեւանի գազանանոցը լրիվ վիրտուալ սարքեք, շատ լավ կլինի։ Հլը որ իսկական կենդանիներին էլ փոխարինեն հոլոգրամներով, լրիվ վերջը կլնի, թե չե որտեղից ասես բերում, սոված պահում են մի հատ խցիկի մեջ, հետո էլ թե սատկեց։

comments.comment_body: Thanks for your reply. I understand the intended meaning, but it still sounds odd to me: the subject of the sentence is "building", and I have never heard/read/seen someone use "degrees" for a building. "Inside, the temperature was 78 degrees" sounds more natural and correct.
comments.post_body:
There were some discrepancies in the "punctuation" chapter quiz, including the double quotes (both of them) being at the end of sentences, but there was a bigger thing that caught my attention:
"Inside, the building was 78 degrees." I am not sure what this even means, can anybody explain?

comments.comment_body: https://www.snopes.com/fact-check/wolf-pack-photo/
comments.post_body:
👌☺️😍✨✨✨ 💐💐💐💞 A Wolf Pack: 😍✨✨✨ 💐💐💐
The first 3 are the old & sick, they walk in front to set the pace of the entire pack. The next 5 are the strongest & best, they are tasked to protect the front side in case of an attack. 
In the center are the rest of the pack members, always protected from any attack. 
The next 5 are also among the strongest; following and tasked to protect the back side. 
Last is alone, the alpha; The LEADER. He controls everything from the rear, ensures that no one is left behind. He is always ready to run in any direction to protect, and serves as the 'bodyguard' to the entire group. 

Just in case anyone wanted to know what it really means to be a leader. It is not about being out front. It means taking care of the team.  🎃🎃🎃
